summary_en: SYLA Holdings raises FY2026 May period-end dividend forecast by ¥1.00 to ¥7.00 per share, driven by upward earnings revision (revenue +13.0%, operating profit +26.4%).
title_en: Notice Regarding Revision of FY2026 May Period Full-Year Consolidated Earnings Forecast and Period-End Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief:
On May 20, 2026, SYLA Holdings Co., Ltd. announced upward revisions to its FY2026 May period consolidated earnings forecast and period-end dividend forecast. The company's board approved these modifications based on recent business performance trends.

Earnings Revision: Full-year revenue is now forecast at ¥39.0 billion (up ¥4.5 billion or +13.0%), operating profit at ¥3.05 billion (+26.4%), and ordinary profit at ¥2.0 billion (+48.1%). Net income attributable to parent company shareholders remains flat at ¥6.513 billion, as improved operational results are offset by special losses including subsidiary liquidation and asset impairment. Q3 cumulative results exceeded plan due to new apartment sales progressing as budgeted and multiple unplanned property sales, including a transaction disclosed on May 15.

Dividend Increase: Period-end dividend per share is raised by ¥1.00 from the prior forecast of ¥6.00 to ¥7.00 per share for FY2026 May (comprised of ¥6.00 ordinary dividend and ¥1.00 commemorative dividend). Annual dividend totals ¥13.00 per share. Management reaffirmed commitment to shareholder returns aligned with profit growth, targeting maintenance or growth of dividends in subsequent periods despite geopolitical supply-chain risks.
